long_description: The kube-etcd-backup-operator-546fbd77dd-fn28s pod in in-che-prod01-zone3-edge is stuck in ImagePullBackOff which is causing promotion tests to fail.
close_notes:
<!-- do-not-process -->
(All Timestamps must be in UTC)
Issue Summary: needed to restart the kube-etcd-backup-operator pod and resync the reqcred
SME Findings:
Reason of Escalation:
Resolution Steps: needed to restart the kube-etcd-backup-operator pod and resync the reqcred
Signature of Issue :unable to start new pods
Jira/Github/Other Tickets: https://ibm-iaas.atlassian.net/browse/INFRA-11741
Category/Subcategory: is-vpc pod failures pod not running
Timeline Summary: found issue / worked with requester who seen this issue before and ran same steps
Resolution Runbook: Not Needed
Caused by Change/Incident:
Warroom: https://ibm-cloudplatform.slack.com/archives/CQWH66SBY/p1765208561073909?thread_ts=1765200947.968969&cid=CQWH66SBY
Meeting URL:
Outage Start:
Outage End :


Other Information: note added requstor to corebot to allow inc create via bot in future
current_status_next_steps:
Current Status:
- kube-etcd-backup-operator-546fbd77dd-fn28s pod in in-che-prod01-zone3-edge is stuck in ImagePullBackOff and needs to be cleaned up.  There is already another kube-etcd-backup-operator running.

Next Steps:
1) Connect to in-che-prod01-zone3-edge

2) Delete the pod to see if this addresses the issue.
- kubectl delete pod -n kube-system kube-etcd-backup-operator-546fbd77dd-fn28s

3) Check if pod has been successfully replaced. (This may take a couple minutes)
- kubectl get pod -n kube-system | grep etcd

regcred -o yaml > kube-system-regcred.yaml\\r\\n\\r\\n2) Edit the kube-system-regcred.yaml. Delete metadata.annotations, metadata.creationTimestamp, metadata.resourceVersion and metadata.uid. Change the namespace from \\\"genctl\\\" to \\\"kube-system\\\".\\r\\n\\r\\n3) Apply the kube-system-regcred.yaml\\r\\n- kubectl apply -f kube-system-regcred.yaml\\r\\n\\r\\n4) Verify that the regcred secret has been created.\\r\\n- kubectl get secret -n kube-system\\r\\n\\r\\n5) Restart the kube-etcd-backup-operator to pick up the regcred secret\\r\\n- kubectl rollout restart -n kube-system deployment.apps/kube-etcd-backup-operator\\r\\n\\r\\n6) Check if pod has been successfully replaced.
